meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Fix Version Control for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how a SaaS starter kit solves version control issues for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: The most effective way to eliminate 'no version control' issues is to adopt a pre-configured SaaS starter kit integrated with Git. By using Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to automate commit hygiene, you ensure every iteration is tracked, reversible, and production-ready for your clients.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: it’s 3:00 AM, the deadline is hours away, and you’ve just nuked your core authentication logic while trying to push a hotfix. Without version control, you aren't just losing code—you’re losing your reputation, your billable hours, and your sanity. Relying on folder copies named 'final_v2_real' is a recipe for professional disaster.

But there is a better way. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you build on a foundation of professional-grade infrastructure that mandates version control from the first line of code. This isn't just about saving files; it's about building a scalable, repeatable engine that turns you from a stressed freelancer into a high-velocity SaaS architect.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Git-Ready Starter Kit
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit that includes a pre-configured .gitignore and local repository setup.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS starter kit foundation using Next.js and Tailwind, ensuring the repository is initialized with Git and a standard .gitignore for Node projects.

steps.title: Configure Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Prevent 'no version control' habits by installing Husky hooks that force you to commit before running local dev builds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a husky configuration that runs a lint-staged check before every commit to ensure code quality and version history integrity.

steps.title: Automate Documentation via Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate meaningful commit messages based on your actual code changes, reducing the friction of tracking updates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current working directory and generate a summary of changes for a commit message following Conventional Commits standards.

steps.title: Implement Branching Strategy
steps.description: Establish a feature-branch workflow to isolate experimental changes from your stable production codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that automates the creation of feature branches and links them to the main project development workflow.

steps.title: Deploy with Continuous Integration
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline so that every push is tested and versioned automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that automates testing and deployment on push to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'touch .github/workflows/deploy.yml && git add . && git commit -m "chore: add ci/cd pipeline"'

pro_tips: Never work on the 'main' branch; treat it as a read-only production environment.
Use 'git stash' to safely park work-in-progress code when a client requests a sudden, urgent pivot.
Tag your releases using semantic versioning (v1.0.0) to give clients a clear sense of progress and stability.
Configure your IDE to show git gutter diffs so you can see exactly what changed before you commit.
Use a 'commit-msg' hook to enforce that every commit message starts with a ticket number, keeping your history audit-ready for clients.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is version-controlled, focus on deploying to a staging environment and sharing the preview link with your client to build trust.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own workflow and start shipping software that stays stable, scalable, and professional. You have the tools, the strategy, and the expertise to dominate your niche. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
